data.body:
### [5/10] bindings.json GROWTH + LEVERS (measured on the live 14.6 MB file, 1235 bindings)

CONTENT BREAKDOWN: baseline = 96.8% of each entry; within baseline, description = 86.1% (stored as the full Jira ADF dict, NOT plain text — mean 5787 B, median 4088 B, max 31,850 B; 6.81 MB total across 1235). status/priority are full Jira objects too. Identity (local_id/jira_key) + bookkeeping ~3%.

DOMINANT GROWTH DRIVER = `baseline_advanced_at`, a WRITE-ONLY timestamp bumped UNCONDITIONALLY on ~all in-window bindings every pass. set_baseline (binding_store.py:371-375) rewrites baseline + stamps baseline_advanced_at = now() with NO "did the value change?" guard; _advance_baselines (reconcile.py:914-930, called :965) loops ALL confirmed in-window bindings every persist pass. PROOF: 1134/1235 entries (92%) share ONE baseline_advanced_at second from the last pass. baseline_advanced_at is NEVER READ anywhere. => bindings.json changes on essentially EVERY pass, editing a scattered timestamp across 92% of a sort-ordered file = pathological for git delta. ~987 versions ~= ~987 passes (NOT "passes where a value changed"), deltas ~222 KB/version.

LEVERS (safest-first, measured whole-file sizes):
1. CHURN GUARD (biggest bang, provably safe, no equality change): drop `baseline_advanced_at` (or bump only on real change) + add a write-when-changed guard in set_baseline so a no-op advance leaves the entry (and the file blob) byte-identical -> git dedups -> ZERO new version. This attacks the VERSION COUNT (the 987), not just per-version size. Without it, a content lever alone still emits ~1 version/pass forever (reclaimed 219 MB returns in ~2 months).
2. TEXT NORMALIZE (-66%: 14.64 MB -> 4.97 MB, near-zero risk, NO migration): store description as adf_to_text(...) and status/priority as `.name` inside set_baseline. Behavior-preserving BY CONSTRUCTION — it stores exactly the projection the comparison already computes via _extract_jira_field (outbound_fields.py:201-234); _extract_jira_field already passes a plain-string baseline straight through, and reads both ADF-dict and text forms, so old+new entries coexist and auto-convert within ~1 pass. Leave `assignee` un-normalized (3.2%, usually null; its dict enables _assignee_matches multi-form tolerance).
3. DIGEST (sha256 of normalized value, -92%: -> 1.14 MB) — OPTIONAL, higher risk (equality-kernel change + migration + one-way value). NOT needed for sustainability; (1)+(2) already make growth sustainable. If pursued, gate exactly like the prev->baseline rollout (dual-write shadow -> N divergent=0 passes -> flag flip -> retire). See [7] for the assignee KILLER.

OTHER: reverse index is derivable but only 0.3% of the file and read directly by _ids.py:67 in ordinary clones -> not worth changing. Retired entries already compacted OUT (popped to separate bindings-retired.json with NO baseline, binding_store.py:428-439) -> do not bloat bindings.json.
